description: Implement DND (Do Not Disturb) mode as a new server module with API endpoints, server-side SSE filtering in the bus, and auto-DND logic for actors in phase 'live' with status 'position'. Management override ensures 'notfall' level always gets through.
steps: Create server/modules/dnd.mod.js following the module pattern from server/modules/live.mod.js and server/modules/announce.mod.js. The module should:
- Store DND state in a 'dnd' collection in the DB with records keyed by personId: { personId, manual: bool, since: timestamp }
- POST /api/dnd/enable: lets the current user (actor, lead, springer) manually enable DND. Stores { personId, manual: true, since: now() }. Publishes 'dnd.changed' via bus.
- POST /api/dnd/disable: lets the current user disable DND. Removes the record. Publishes 'dnd.changed'.
- GET /api/dnd/status: returns { active: bool, manual: bool, auto: bool } for the current user. Auto-DND is computed: phase === 'live' AND presence.actorStatus === 'position'.
- GET /api/dnd/status/:personId: management/lead can query any person's DND status.

- Export a helper function isDND(db, personId) that returns true if the person has manual DND active OR auto-DND conditions are met (phase 'live' + actorStatus 'position'). This function is imported by the bus filtering logic.
Modify server/kernel/bus.js to support a DND filter layer:
- Add a setDndCheck(fn) method on Bus that accepts a function (personId) => boolean. This is called by the dnd module at init time.

- In the publish() method, after the audience filter passes, apply an additional DND check: if the event type is 'announce.new' and the announcement data.level is NOT 'notfall', skip delivery to clients where dndCheck(client.person.id) returns true. Also skip 'alarm' events unless data.level === 'notfall'. All other event types pass through normally (presence, chat, tasks, settings etc are never DND-filtered).
- DND never blocks events of type 'alarm' where data.level === 'notfall' -- these always go through (management Notfall override).
Register the dnd module in the kernel's module loading (it auto-discovers *.mod.js files in server/modules/, so just creating the file is enough).
Add 'dnd.changed' to the TOPIC_BY_EVENT map in web/js/core/store.js so the frontend can react to DND state changes.
Wire the dnd module's isDND helper into the bus at server startup. The module's routes() function receives { db, bus } -- use bus.setDndCheck((pid) => isDND(db, pid)) inside the routes() init.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/dnd/enable sets DND for the calling user and returns { ok: true, active: true }
POST /api/dnd/disable removes DND for the calling user and returns { ok: true, active: false }
When DND is active for an actor, bus.publish of 'announce.new' with level 'info' or 'wichtig' does NOT reach that actor's SSE stream
When DND is active, bus.publish of 'announce.new' with level 'notfall' STILL reaches the actor (management override)
Auto-DND triggers when phase is 'live' and actorStatus is 'position' (without manual toggle)
Other event types (presence.changed, chat.message, task.changed, etc.) are never filtered by DND
npm test passes with new DND tests added
verification: Run `npm test` and confirm all tests pass including new DND section
